conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, I need to ask: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me how many children you have and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and lives at home with you.

Since Alex is over 16, his eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether he's in approved education or training. **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he in full-time non-advanced education, such as A-levels, or an approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is! He's studying for his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is studying for his A-levels, that would typically count as approved education.

Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you currently live with Alex, and are you responsible for him?** (For example, do you provide for his day-to-day needs like food, clothes, and accommodation?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. He lives with me and I provide everything for him - food, clothes, accommodation, all of it.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, another parent or guardian?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 18 years old and studying for his A-levels, which counts as approved education
- You can claim Child Benefit for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training
- You live in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives with you
- You're responsible for him and provide for all his needs
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Important note:** Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. Even if this applies, you can still claim Child Benefit - you would just need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government process. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and studying for his A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. You live in the UK (Manchester), Alex lives with you at home, you are responsible for him and provide for all his needs (food, clothes, accommodation), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 18, who lives at home with them in Manchester. Alex is currently studying for his A-levels (approved education). The claimant is responsible for Alex, provides for all his needs, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.
